text.title: Agent Failure Modes When Using This Index
text.summary: Known ways agents misuse this index and how to prevent them.

antipattern.description: Agent reads the 5-line code samples in this index and treats them as complete implementations, producing shallow output without loading sibling content.
antipattern.reason: This index was intentionally kept as stubs. Implementing from stubs is guaranteed to miss edge cases, error handling, and project conventions documented in siblings.

rule.statement: Force a sibling read before generating any code. Reject prompts that quote this index's stubs instead of the sibling content.

antipattern.description: Agent calls Task(subagent_type="faion-code-agent", ...) or similar because the index lists that name.
antipattern.reason: These agent names are aspirational. The call fails at runtime. Only faion-sdd-executor-agent, nero-sdd-executor-agent, and password-scrubber-agent exist.

antipattern.description: Agent quotes the generic "Next.js App Router" or "React Component Pattern" snippets from the legacy README and ships 2-year-old patterns.
antipattern.reason: Current guidance is in best-practices-2026. The legacy index predates React 19, TypeScript 5, and Python 3.12.

antipattern.description: Agent trusts the headline "68 methodologies" figure and assumes it reflects the current sibling folder count.
antipattern.reason: Use ls to count sibling folders; the headline figure drifts as new methodologies are added or removed.

antipattern.description: Agent routes Kubernetes / Terraform work through this software-developer index instead of pro/infra/ siblings.
antipattern.reason: DevOps patterns here predate the pro/infra/* skill expansion and are stub-quality. Use the correct tier.
